data.last_assistant_message:
Zero TypeScript errors. All changes are complete:

---

**Summary of changes:**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Removed `--webpack` from `dev` and `build` → Turbopack in dev, SWC in prod |
| `src/components/MessageRenderer/CodeBlock/index.tsx` | Lazy-loaded `react-syntax-highlighter` via `next/dynamic` (ssr: false) |
| `src/lib/models/registry.ts` | Exported `modelRegistry` singleton at module level |
| `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/search/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/providers/route.ts` | Uses singleton (mutation routes — critical for consistency) |
| `src/app/api/providers/[id]/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/providers/[id]/models/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/suggestions/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/images/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/videos/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/uploads/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/app/api/config/route.ts` | Uses singleton |
| `src/lib/verification/textSimilarity.ts` | Eliminated redundant `normalizeText` in sliding window — O(n×m) → O(window_size) |

**Key note on singleton scope:** The plan called for updating only `chat` and `search`, but the mutation routes (`addProvider`, `removeProvider`, `updateProvider`) also needed the singleton to stay in sync — otherwise adding/removing a provider via the settings UI would update the config file but not the singleton, breaking subsequent chat requests. All 10 routes now share the same instance.
